seoTitle: Add a Date Slicer in Power BI to Filter by Time Fast
metaDescription: Learn how to add and customize a date slicer in Power BI so viewers can filter reports by time period quickly and interactively.
intro: Time-based filtering is one of the most useful features you can add to a Power BI report, letting viewers zoom into exactly the period that matters to them. This guide covers adding a date slicer, customizing its settings, and using it to make your reports more dynamic.

steps.heading: Add the date slicer
steps.body: Insert a date slicer visual into your report, connecting it to the relevant date field in your data model.

steps.heading: Customize your slicer settings
steps.body: Adjust the slicer's format, whether that's a range selector, dropdown, or relative date filter, to match how viewers will interact with it.

steps.heading: Filter data efficiently over specific time ranges
steps.body: Use the slicer to test filtering across different time ranges, confirming that your visuals update correctly as the selected period changes.

steps.heading: Integrate AI features for deeper insight
steps.body: Pair time-based filtering with Power BI's AI-driven tools to support predictive analysis and more responsive real-time reporting.

steps.heading: Apply date slicers to real business use cases
steps.body: Time-based filtering supports financial analysis, sales forecasting, and project tracking, where seeing performance across specific periods drives better decisions.

faq.question: Can a date slicer filter multiple visuals on the same page at once?
faq.answer: Yes, by default a slicer filters all the visuals on the same report page that are connected to the relevant date field.

faq.question: What's the difference between a date range slicer and a relative date filter?
faq.answer: A range slicer lets users manually select a start and end date, while a relative date filter automatically adjusts based on a rolling period like the last 30 days.

faq.question: Do I need a dedicated date table to use a date slicer?
faq.answer: It's best practice to use a proper date table in your data model, since it improves filtering accuracy and supports more advanced time intelligence.

faq.question: Can I apply a date slicer across multiple report pages?
faq.answer: Yes, Power BI allows you to sync a slicer across multiple pages so the same time filter applies consistently throughout the report.
